prevalence.source: Hematology epidemiology syntheses (AIHA and hereditary hemolytic anemias)

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; can be severe in acute hemolysis or chronic transfusion dependence
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate depending on etiology and comorbidities
burden.source: IHME anemia and hematologic disease context

vulnerablePopulations: patients with autoimmune disease or malignancy
patients with limited access to hematology care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Anemia with hemolysis markers (↑LDH, ↑indirect bilirubin, ↓haptoglobin, reticulocytosis), peripheral smear; direct antiglobulin test (DAT/Coombs) for immune hemolysis; evaluate underlying cause

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; evaluated when symptomatic or abnormal CBC found
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; often good with timely diagnosis and targeted therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC and hemolysis work-up available in larger labs; DAT and specialized testing may be delayed in smaller municipalities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause; corticosteroids first-line for warm AIHA, immunosuppressants/rituximab when indicated; transfusion support; folate; splenectomy in select cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Specialized hematology access and availability of advanced immunotherapies varies by region
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core diagnostics and steroids/transfusion support broadly available
